title: Create Express route file for userId endpoint
description: Add a new file (e.g., routes/userId.js) that defines an Express router and exports it for use in the main app.

title: Implement route handler to return static user ID and status
description: Write a GET handler (e.g., '/userId') that responds with JSON containing a hardcoded user ID (e.g., { userId: '12345', status: 'ok' }) and ensures no database or external calls are made.

title: Integrate the new route into the main Express application
description: Import the router in the server entry point (e.g., app.js or server.js) and mount it under an appropriate path (e.g., app.use('/', userIdRouter)).

title: Verify route returns correct response format
description: Start the server locally, send a request to the endpoint (using curl, Postman, or similar), and confirm the response includes the user ID and status fields with HTTP 200 OK.
